persona: Solo founder-operator (the agency itself) — weekly, time-poor, pops cold into each client cockpit to curate fast and stand behind what the client sees.
currentFriction: THE COCKPIT AND THE CLIENT NOW DISAGREE ABOUT WHAT THE PRODUCT IS. The client opens on a dollar verdict ('23 form fills · 41 calls ≈ ~$11,000 vs your $1,500 retainer, up from 6 when we started'). The cockpit (KeywordStrategy.tsx issueOverviewEl) opens on IssueHeader → StanceBar → DraftedPovEditor → BackingMovesQueue — a recommendation-curation list. I curate the INPUT (recs + prose) but never see the OUTPUT the client leads with. I can ship an issue without ever having looked at my own headline number.

THE NUMBER I AM ON THE HOOK FOR LIVES IN A DIFFERENT BUILDING. outcomeValue, segment, and (P1a) event-pinning + the Webflow webhook all live in settings/ClientDashboardTab.tsx — a separate tab I have to remember to visit. The persona watch-item ('the number is only as honest as one admin input — a misset value or wrong pinned events yields a confident-but-wrong estimate') is precisely the input I CANNOT see while curating. The integrity of the headline is divorced from the weekly flow that produces the headline.

POV PROSE IS NARRATED AROUND THE WRONG NOUN. DraftedPovEditor's fields are Situation / 'The one move I'd bring' / 'Wins worth saying' / 'What I'd flag' — all recommendation-flavored. The client's hero is now a measured outcome delta vs a baseline vs spend. My prose can wax about an authority_bet while the client stares at '14 new patients, up from 6.' I'm writing the caption to a different photo.

RECS ARE FRAMED BY THE OLD CURRENCY. CockpitRow tags are [severity][value][lifecycle] and StanceBar groups by archetype (authority_bet/refresh_reclaim/defend/...). Nothing on a row says 'this move grows the outcome count' or 'projected +N leads / +~$X.' I curate by SEO taxonomy; the client buys outcomes. I can't sort or stage by 'what moves the number.'

STAGED ≠ MEASURED, AND I CAN'T SEE THE GAP. The header counter reads 'N staged · M already with client' (IssueHeader/BackingMovesQueue) — a curation count. It says nothing about whether tracking is confirmed, whether provenance will render as estimate_ga4 vs measured_action, or whether the verdict will even compute. I can 'Send issue' into a workspace whose verdict says 'establishing your baseline now' and not know it.

NO PREVIEW-AS-CLIENT. IssueHeader's own header comment notes the preview-as-client toggle was REMOVED in Phase 1 ('no client preview surface to switch to'). So at the exact moment the client experience became a high-stakes dollar claim, I lost the ability to see what I'm about to send. I curate blind to the deliverable.

P1A ADDS A NEW INTEGRITY JOB WITH NO HOME IN THE FLOW. Pinning + typing GA4 events, pasting the Webflow webhook, confirming setup (conversionTrackingConfirmedAt), and reading the verification readout ('3 events pinned · 2 typed · forms connected · last lead 2h ago') all land in ClientDashboardTab — a setup screen I touch once and forget. There's no recurring nudge in the cockpit when tracking drifts (last lead 30d ago, an event got unpinned, value is unset).
reframeNeeds: A VERDICT MIRROR AT THE TOP OF THE COCKPIT. Before I curate anything I must see the exact sentence the client will see, with its provenance label (estimate_ga4 vs measured_action), its baseline anchor, and its dollar band — rendered from the same baselineVerdict()/computeROI().outcomeVerdict the client reads. 'Pop in cold' should mean 'land on the number, then curate toward it,' not 'land on a rec list and hope the number is fine.'

AN INTEGRITY STRIP I CAN'T SEND AROUND. A compact readout that tells me, in the cockpit, whether this issue's number is honest: outcomeValue set? (basis), segment confirmed?, events pinned + typed?, Webflow forms connected + last lead freshness?, baseline exists?, provenance tier. With one-click jumps to fix each. The send button should surface a soft warning when the verdict will render thin/estimate-only ('Heads up: no lead value set — client sees a count-only verdict').

POV CO-AUTHORED WITH THE NUMBER. The drafted POV must be seeded FROM the verdict (lead with the outcome delta, then the moves that earned it), and a staleness nudge when the curated moves no longer explain the number. Reframe the lead field from 'The one move I'd bring' toward 'Why the number moved / what moves it next.'

OUTCOME-DENOMINATED REC FRAMING. Let me see and stage recs by projected outcome impact ('+~N leads', 'defends ~$X of current capture'), not just severity/value/archetype. StanceBar should be able to read as 'this issue's mix of growth vs defense of the number,' not only an archetype split. I curate the OUTCOME STORY, so the substrate must speak in outcomes.

PREVIEW-AS-CLIENT RESTORED AS A FIRST-CLASS CONTROL. A real toggle/side-by-side that renders TheIssueClientPage with this workspace's live data so I sign off on the actual deliverable before Send issue. The send button should mean 'I've seen what they'll see.'

A RECURRING TRACKING-HEALTH SIGNAL IN THE WEEKLY FLOW. Because I own the integrity of the number, the cockpit (not just Settings) must nudge me when tracking decays: stale webhook, unpinned conversion event, value unset, baseline missing. Make the once-and-forget P1a setup a thing the weekly curation loop keeps honest.
keep: The single-commit send model — staging-only verbs + the ONE canonical 'Send issue' button in IssueHeader, with numerator/denominator sharing one source (cockpitRecs + isCuratedForClient). It's the discipline that prevents accidental client writes; keep it exactly, just teach the counter to also speak to the verdict's readiness.

The 5-beat spine shape (header → stance → POV → queue → send) and the 'open to the decision, not a wall' collapse of everything else into one 'Supporting detail' disclosure. The skeleton is right for an outcome cockpit; only the content of beats 1–3 needs to graduate from recs to the number.

Inline editable POV with the cut→sentence live contract and the generatedAt-only draft reset (the lost-keystroke guard in DraftedPovEditor). Editable narrative is exactly the muscle the outcome story needs — reuse it, repoint it.

The cut/park/stage/edit-wording verbs and CockpitRow's commit-on-blur wording overrides. Operator steering over wording is more important than ever when the client reads a dollar claim — keep it, extend it to outcome framing.

The honesty engineering the personas cited as the win: provenance labels, fmtEstimate() banding, no fabricated baseline/delta, the 'establishing your baseline now' degradation. The cockpit must mirror this honesty, never override it — the operator should see the SAME labeled estimate, never a falsely-precise admin-only number.

ClientDashboardTab's basis-precedence outcomeValue input and the read-only/overridable segment resolution. The data model is right; it just needs to be surfaced into the cockpit, not relocated wholesale.
change: Add an OutcomeVerdictMirror as the new beat 1 of issueOverviewEl, ABOVE IssueHeader's curation chrome — render the client's exact verdict sentence + provenance tier + baseline + dollar band from the same server seam (computeROI().outcomeVerdict / baselineVerdict()). This is the 'pop in cold, see the number' fix.

Add a NumberIntegrityStrip (compact, dismissable-to-detail) between the verdict mirror and StanceBar: outcomeValue/basis · segment · events pinned+typed · Webflow forms connected + last-lead freshness · baseline status · resolved provenance — each a one-click deep link into the relevant ClientDashboardTab section. Promote the P1a verification readout OUT of Settings into this strip.

Wire a send-time guard into IssueHeader's disabledReason/secondary-text: when the verdict will render estimate-only, count-only (no lead value), or thin (no baseline), show a non-blocking warning beside 'Send issue' ('Client will see a count-only verdict — set a lead value?'). Never block; just stop me sending a weaker number than I think.

Repoint DraftedPovEditor's lead field and POV generation to the verdict: seed the POV from the outcome delta first, moves second; rename/relabel 'The one move I'd bring' toward 'What moved the number / what moves it next'; extend the staleness nudge to fire when the curated mix no longer explains the verdict.

Add projected-outcome framing to the rec substrate: a per-rec outcome-impact tag on CockpitRow (e.g. '+~N leads' / 'defends ~$X') and an outcome lens on StanceBar (growth-vs-defense-of-the-number), so staging can be driven by 'what grows the verdict,' not only archetype/severity.
Restore preview-as-client as a first-class toggle/side-by-side in IssueHeader that renders the live TheIssueClientPage for this workspace, so Send issue means 'I've seen the deliverable.'

Pull the P1a conversion-tracking setup (event pin+type, webhook connect, confirm) into a cockpit-reachable flow (modal or the integrity strip's deep links), and add a recurring cockpit nudge when tracking decays (stale webhook / unpinned event / unset value / missing baseline) so the once-set setup stays honest week over week.
Re-label the header counter so it speaks to outcome readiness, not just curation: pair 'N staged · M with client' with a verdict-state chip ('measured' / 'estimate' / 'baseline pending').
thesis: The admin job must flip from 'curate the recommendation list' to 'produce and stand behind the client's dollar verdict' — which means the cockpit has to open on the same number the client opens on, expose the integrity of that number inline, and let me curate the moves and the prose toward it. Today the operator curates the inputs while structurally blind to the output and its honesty; the reframe is to make the verdict (and the one admin input it lives or dies by) the first thing I see and the last thing I sign off on.
killerQuestion: When I 'pop in cold' and hit Send issue, can I see — on this one screen, without leaving the cockpit — the exact dollar/outcome verdict my client will read, whether it's a measured number or just an estimate, and whether it's honest right now (lead value set, right events pinned and typed, Webflow connected, baseline real) — or am I still shipping a number I've never actually looked at?

persona: Scaling operator (20-40 clients) — portfolio/triage lens. Runs a book of cockpits; needs to know which clients have a weakening dollar verdict, which are unmeasured/unconfigured, and where to spend curation time — without opening 30 cockpits one at a time.
currentFriction: The cockpit is single-workspace by construction. IssueHeader (src/components/strategy/issue/IssueHeader.tsx) and the whole `issueOverviewEl` spine in KeywordStrategy.tsx are scoped to one workspaceId. At 30 clients there is no portfolio entry point to The Issue — I must open each cockpit serially to learn anything. The new client direction made the number the product, but the admin still has zero cross-client view of that number.

The admin cockpit is BLIND to the dollar verdict it now exists to grow. grep across src/components/strategy for outcomeVerdict|provenance|conversionTrackingConfirmedAt|outcomeValue|measured_action is EMPTY. The client opens on a $11k verdict; the operator curating that client sees archetype mix (StanceBar) and a rec queue (BackingMovesQueue) framed by severity/value tags — never the number the client is staring at. I am curating wording for an outcome I cannot see.

A cross-client surface EXISTS but is denominated in the WRONG units. OutcomesOverview (src/components/admin/outcomes/OutcomesOverview.tsx, backed by WorkspaceOutcomeOverview in shared/types/outcome-tracking.ts:339 and GET /api/outcomes/overview) ranks workspaces by winRate / activeActions / scoredLast30d / LearningsTrend / topWin. None of those is the client's dollar verdict. 'Win rate 62%, 14 actions tracked' is an internal-mechanics triage; it cannot tell me which client's $ number DROPPED or is about to embarrass us in front of a CFO.

There is no setup/integrity triage at book scale. P1a makes the number only as honest as one admin input (valuePerOutcome set? events pinned + typed? Webflow webhook confirmed? conversionTrackingConfirmedAt set?). Those live per-workspace in ClientDashboardTab.tsx + the new the-issue-conversion-tracking routes. Nothing rolls them up. At 30 clients I cannot answer 'which 9 are still on estimate_ga4 because I never finished setup' without clicking into 30 Settings tabs. The single most operator-relevant new fact has no portfolio readout.

The provenance tier — the thing that decides whether a client sees ~$11k (estimate) or '23 form fills tracked on your site' (measured) — is invisible to me across the book. I cannot see, in one place, who graduated to measured_action and who is stuck at estimate_ga4, so I cannot prioritize the setup work that converts trust-as-direction into trust-as-proof.

'Attention needed' on the portfolio is computed from outcome win-rate mechanics, not from the verdict the client experiences. A client whose verdict went flat or down, or whose tracking silently broke (last lead 6 days ago), can read 'On track' green because winRate is fine. The portfolio alarm is wired to the wrong signal under the new direction.
reframeNeeds: A portfolio triage denominated in the CLIENT'S verdict, not internal mechanics. Reframe WorkspaceOutcomeOverview + OutcomesOverview to carry, per client: current dollar verdict (outcomeVerdict.estimatedValue), the baseline delta (verdict up/flat/down vs engagement-start), provenance tier (estimate_ga4 | measured_action | actual_reconciled), and a setup-state. The book sorts by 'verdict weakening' and 'verdict highest-$-at-risk', not by win rate.

A setup/integrity column at book scale — the operator's new job made triageable. One readout per client: outcomeValue set? events pinned & typed (N pinned · M typed)? Webflow forms connected? conversionTrackingConfirmedAt set? last lead age? This is the cross-client version of the per-cockpit verification readout the P1a plan specs for ClientDashboardTab. Without it the new operator responsibility doesn't scale past ~8 clients.

A provenance ladder rollup: how many of my book are still estimate_ga4 (unmeasured), how many measured_action, how many reconciled — so I can run a 'graduate the book to measured' campaign instead of discovering one client at a time that their number is still an estimate.

An integrity alarm denominated in the number's trustworthiness, not just its trend. Flag the failure modes the personas named: stale capture (GA4 says 47 / webhook captured 12 — the reconcileFormCountVsGa4 discrepancy already computed in server/form-submissions.ts), a misset valuePerOutcome, unpinned/untyped events producing a confident-wrong estimate, a verdict that went DOWN. These must surface at portfolio level, because a confident-wrong number is the churn trigger and I own its integrity now.

Inside the single cockpit: the verdict the client sees must be the operator's North Star, present at the top of IssueHeader. Curation decisions (keep/cut/park/stage) should be legible as 'moves that grow THIS number', with each rec carrying a projected $/outcome contribution (the spec's NextBetsCard idea, re-expressed in dollars via outcomeValue) — so I curate the dollar narrative, not just archetype mix and wording.

A drill path: portfolio row (verdict + setup state + integrity flag) → click → the cockpit, which now opens on that same verdict. One denomination from book to cockpit so triage and curation speak the same language.
keep: The single-commit send discipline. The word 'send' living in exactly one place (IssueHeader), per-row/bulk actions that only STAGE, the atomic bulk-send route, and numerator/denominator sharing one source (cockpitRecs + isCuratedForClient). This is the operator's safety rail; a portfolio reframe must not introduce a second send path or a cross-client bulk-send that bypasses per-client curation.

The curation verb set and cascade safety — keep/cut(strike with cascade confirm)/park(throttle, resurfaces in Nd)/stage/inline edit wording + edit POV. The cut→sentence contract (cutting the lead move's backing card removes its POV sentence live) and the staleness nudge. These are the craft of curation and survive any reframe untouched.

DraftedPovEditor as the narrated point of view (Situation / the one move / wins worth saying / what I'd flag) with optimistic debounced PATCH + Regenerate. The outcome-narrative reframe BUILDS ON this — it's already the place the operator authors a story; it just needs the dollar verdict anchored above it.

The existing cross-client substrate itself: OutcomesOverview + WorkspaceOutcomeOverview + GET /api/outcomes/overview + useOutcomeOverview + the attentionNeeded/attentionReason + sort-attention-first pattern. Reframe this surface in place rather than building a net-new portfolio page — the table shell, drill-to-workspace, and attention-sort UX are the right bones.

StanceBar's proportional archetype mix as a SECONDARY read. It is still useful for understanding curation balance; it just must stop being the primary frame now that the dollar verdict is the headline.

StrategyConfigPanel mounted as page chrome (not tab-buried) and the freshness subtitle ('Generated 12 Jun · 48 pages mapped'). Config-as-chrome was the right call; the setup-state readout should join it as chrome, not hide in Settings.
Flag-OFF byte-identical discipline and one-PR-per-phase. Any reframe ships gated and OFF-identical, consistent with how the client side shipped.
change: Reframe OutcomesOverview into 'The Book' — a verdict-denominated portfolio triage. Extend WorkspaceOutcomeOverview (shared/types/outcome-tracking.ts:339) with: currentVerdictValue, baselineDelta (verdict direction vs engagement-start), provenance (OutcomeProvenance), and setupState. Extend GET /api/outcomes/overview to populate them from computeROI().outcomeVerdict per workspace. Default sort: verdict weakening / highest-$-at-risk first — replacing the winRate sort. Keep winRate as a secondary column, not the headline.

Add a setup-integrity column/readout to every portfolio row: a compact 'valueSet · N pinned · M typed · forms connected · last lead 2h' badge derived from outcomeValue + eventConfig.outcomeType + getFormCaptureStatus()/conversionTrackingConfirmedAt. A client with no outcomeValue or zero typed events renders 'Setup incomplete — estimate only', linking straight to that client's ClientDashboardTab conversion-tracking section.

Add a provenance ladder summary strip at the top of The Book (alongside the existing AggregateStats): 'Book: 9 estimate · 14 measured · 0 reconciled' so the operator can drive a graduate-to-measured campaign across the portfolio. Make each segment clickable to filter the table.

Wire a portfolio integrity alarm: surface reconcileFormCountVsGa4 discrepancies (GA4 vs captured), stale-capture (last lead age over threshold), verdict-down, and 'estimate-only after N days live' as attentionReason values — so attentionNeeded reflects the number's trustworthiness, not just win-rate mechanics. This is the single highest-leverage change for the portfolio lens.

Inside the cockpit, hoist the client's dollar verdict to the top of IssueHeader as the operator North Star ('This client now sees: 14 new patients ≈ ~$11k vs $1,500 — up from 9 · estimate_ga4'), with a one-click 'finish setup to make it measured' affordance when provenance is estimate_ga4. The operator must see what the client sees while curating.

Give each CockpitRow a projected $/outcome contribution tag (reuse rec estimatedGain × outcomeValue — the NextBetsCard math the persona review flagged as a P0 miss). Reframe BackingMovesQueue grouping/labels from archetype-first to 'moves that grow the number', archetype as secondary metadata. Curation becomes dollar-narrative curation.

Add a portfolio entry point to nav (a 'Book / Outcomes' surface) that is the default operator landing when running many clients, with drill-through that opens each cockpit on its verdict — so the book and the cockpit share one denomination.
thesis: The admin job must be reframed from 'curate a recommendation list, one cockpit at a time' to 'operate a book of provable dollar verdicts' — triaging at the portfolio level by which client's number is weakening or unmeasured, and curating at the cockpit level toward that same verdict. The cross-client surface already exists (OutcomesOverview); it just needs to be re-denominated from internal win-rate mechanics into the client-facing verdict + setup-integrity state, because the operator now owns the integrity of the number, not just the rec feed.
killerQuestion: When a client's dollar verdict drops this week — or their tracking silently breaks so GA4 says 47 leads and the captured webhook says 12 — does the operator running 30 clients find out from a portfolio triage before the client does, or only by opening that one cockpit by luck?

persona: New-client onboarding operator — the person who just signed a client and now has to make the dollar verdict real: pin GA4 events, type them to outcomes, paste the Webflow webhook, set the lead/customer value, confirm the segment, then curate and send the first Issue.
currentFriction: SETUP IS A SCAVENGER HUNT ACROSS TWO PAGES. The thing that makes the number provable (Outcome Value, Segment, Event Pinning, and the coming GA4-type-mapping + Webflow webhook) lives in Workspace Settings > Client Dashboard as the 4th-6th stacked SectionCards (ClientDashboardTab.tsx lines 727-1121). The thing I actually open every day — the cockpit — is on a different page (KeywordStrategy.tsx issueOverviewEl). Nothing connects them. As an onboarding operator I have no map: no 'do these 4 things before you can send an honest Issue', no ordering, no progress.

THE COCKPIT IS BLIND TO THE NUMBER IT'S CURATING. The client now opens on a dollar verdict, but the cockpit shows recs framed by archetype/severity/generic 'value' tag and never the projected $ impact (confirmed: StanceBar groups by archetype; CockpitRow has no dollar). I'm curating a 'mix of moves' while the client reads '23 new patients = ~$11k'. I literally cannot see the headline I'm responsible for.

I CAN SHIP A BROKEN VERDICT WITH ZERO WARNING. The only send gate is canSend={stagedCount > 0} (KeywordStrategy.tsx:591). If outcomeValue is unset the client sees a count-only verdict with no dollar; if the segment is wrong the wrong inserts render; if tracking is unconfirmed the number stays a soft estimate — and the cockpit lets me hit 'Send issue' anyway. There is no 'this client isn't set up to receive a dollar verdict yet' state anywhere near the send button.

THE OUTCOME VALUE INPUT IS A FREE-TEXT FOOTGUN WITH NO PROOF. ovValue is a bare number field, ovUnit a free-text string ('new patient'), basis defaults to agency_estimate. The persona watch-item is exactly this: one misset value yields a confident-but-wrong client headline. The form gives me no sanity band (is $800/patient sane?), no 'last 90d this would have shown ~$Y' preview, and no echo of what the client will literally read. I'm typing the most load-bearing number in the product into an unvalidated box and flying blind on its downstream effect.

P1a SETUP IS PLANNED INTO THE SAME BURIED TAB, NOT THE OPERATOR'S WORKFLOW. The P1a plan (Lane C) adds the GA4 outcome-type mapping, the Webflow webhook connect UI, and the 3-step checklist all inside ClientDashboardTab.tsx — i.e. deeper in the same Settings card stack. So the act that graduates the number from estimate to 'measured, tracked on your site' is a thing I have to remember to go find in Settings, with no prompt from the cockpit where the actual send happens.

NO READOUT OF THE NUMBER'S HEALTH. There's no single place that tells me: value set? (basis), segment confirmed?, events pinned & typed?, webhook connected?, last lead captured?, what the client's verdict currently renders as. The collapsed summary rows are per-card and per-fact; nobody assembles 'is this client's number honest and live right now'.
reframeNeeds: A SINGLE 'NUMBER READINESS' SURFACE the operator can reach from the cockpit — one card that states, in order: lead/customer value (basis + what the client will read), segment (confirmed), conversions pinned & typed (N/M), Webflow capture connected, last lead captured. This is the P1a 'verification readout' but promoted from a buried card to the operator's spine, and extended to cover value+segment, not just events.

THE SEND BUTTON MUST KNOW ABOUT SETUP. canSend has to graduate from 'stagedCount > 0' to 'staged AND this client can receive the verdict it's about to claim'. At minimum a non-blocking but loud banner: 'No outcome value set — client will see a count-only verdict' / 'Tracking unconfirmed — number ships as an estimate, not measured'. The operator should never discover post-send that the headline was hollow.

THE COCKPIT MUST SHOW THE DOLLAR. The operator needs to see, live, what the client's verdict currently resolves to ('~$11k, estimate, up from ~$4k at start') sitting above or beside the curation queue, and ideally a per-rec '$ this move is meant to grow' framing so curation is denominated the same way the client's value is. Curating mix while the client reads dollars is the core mismatch to kill.

A GUIDED, ORDERED FIRST-RUN SETUP for a freshly-signed client — not six independent collapsed cards. Use the existing OnboardingChecklist pattern (it already takes ordered steps with completed/onClick/estimatedTime): 'Set the value of one outcome', 'Confirm the segment', 'Pin & type your key conversions', 'Connect Webflow form capture', 'Confirm a measured lead landed', 'Curate & send the first Issue'. Each step deep-links to the right control. The operator should know what 'done' looks like before they ever touch the cockpit.

PROVENANCE LITERACY AT THE INPUT. Wherever the operator sets value and confirms tracking, show the consequence in the client's own words and label: 'Right now your client reads: ~$11,000 (estimate). Confirm tracking to graduate this to: 23 form fills · 41 calls, tracked on your site (measured).' Make the estimate→measured ladder a thing the operator can see themselves climbing — that's the unlock the personas asked for, surfaced to the person who controls it.

INTEGRITY GUARDRAILS ON THE LOAD-BEARING INPUTS. The outcome-value form needs a sanity band and a 'preview against last 90d' so a fat-fingered $8,000 (vs $800) is caught before it becomes a client headline. A typed-event mapping that's empty or ambiguous should warn. The operator now owns the integrity of the number; the UI has to help them not corrupt it.
keep: The single canonical 'Send issue' button + live counter + inline disabled-reason (IssueHeader.tsx). The one-send-surface discipline and numerator/denominator-from-one-source rule are exactly right and must survive — just teach the disabled-reason to also speak setup-readiness.

Config-as-page-chrome (StrategyConfigPanel mounted in IssueHeader, not tab-buried). Same instinct should extend to setup: the prerequisites for an honest verdict deserve to be chrome the operator can't miss, not a deep tab.

The P0 Outcome Value card's honesty framing — basis precedence (client_provided > agency_estimate > ai_enriched), the explicit 'always shown as a labeled estimate, never booked revenue' caption, the 'Estimate with AI' assist, and the collapsed summary that already says 'No outcome value set — the client sees a count-only verdict.' That last line is the seed of the readiness model; it just needs to be promoted next to the send button instead of buried.

The deterministic local/multi segment axis being read-only with only the non-local 3-way overridable (ClientDashboardTab.tsx:108-110). Right scoping — don't let operators override what's provably detected.
The Event Display & Pinning model (pin / rename / group / default page). It's the substrate the P1a outcome-type mapping rides on; keep it, add the 'map to outcome type' axis rather than rebuilding.
The whole stage-vs-send local-union model and lifecycle verbs (keep/cut/park/stage/edit). Curation mechanics are good; they just need to be denominated in outcome/$ rather than archetype-only.

P1a's hard honesty rule: provenance graduates to measured_action ONLY on confirmed setup (conversionTrackingConfirmedAt). Never claim 'measured' the instant a flag flips. This is the integrity spine — keep it absolute and make the operator feel the gate, don't hide it.
change: Add a 'Number readiness' panel to the cockpit spine (between IssueHeader and StanceBar, or as a persistent strip in IssueHeader). It assembles one read: value set (basis) · segment confirmed · N/M conversions pinned & typed · Webflow capture connected · last lead Xh ago · CURRENT CLIENT VERDICT = '~$11k (estimate)'. Each row deep-links to its control in ClientDashboardTab. This is the P1a verification readout, promoted from Settings to the cockpit and widened to value+segment.

Upgrade canSend / disabledReason to be setup-aware. Keep the single button. When outcomeValue is null: ship-allowed but with a loud inline 'Client will see a count-only verdict — set an outcome value to show the dollar.' When tracking unconfirmed: 'Number ships as estimate, not measured.' Make these the same inline disabled-reason mechanism that already exists (IssueHeader.tsx:65-69) — extend it, don't add a new pattern.

Show the dollar in the curation surface. Render the live client verdict near the BackingMovesQueue, and add an optional '$ impact' framing to CockpitRow so the operator curates against the same denomination the client reads. At minimum, tag moves that grow the tracked-outcome count distinctly from generic 'value'.

Replace the six independent collapsed setup SectionCards with an ordered, progress-bearing setup flow for new clients, reusing OnboardingChecklist's step model (id/label/description/completed/onClick/estimatedTime). Trigger it on first cockpit visit for a workspace whose readiness is incomplete; each step deep-links to value/segment/pinning/webhook controls. Keep the individual cards as the edit destinations, but give them a spine.

Build the P1a Lane C setup flow as a cockpit-reachable wizard, not just a Settings card. Keep the webhook receiver + admin endpoints where the plan puts them, but surface the 3-step Webflow checklist, the type-mapping, and the 'confirm setup' action from a place the operator hits on the send path — and have the 'Confirm a measured lead landed' step light up the readiness panel and graduate the verdict.

Add integrity guardrails to the outcome-value form: a plausibility band for value-per-outcome, a 'with this value, last 90d would have read ~$Y' preview, and an explicit echo of the client-facing sentence the operator is about to make true. Warn on empty/ambiguous outcome-type mappings before 'confirm setup' can flip conversionTrackingConfirmedAt.

Add provenance-ladder copy at the point of control: 'Now: ~$11,000 (estimate). Confirm tracking → 23 form fills · 41 calls, tracked on your site (measured).' Make the estimate→measured graduation visible and operator-driven.
thesis: The admin job must shift from 'curate a list of recommendations and send it' to 'own the integrity of one dollar number and the moves that grow it' — which means the cockpit has to absorb setup-readiness and the live client verdict, not leave them stranded in a separate Settings tab. The number the client now opens on is the operator's deliverable; the cockpit should make the operator see it, prove it, and be unable to ship a hollow one by accident.
killerQuestion: When an onboarding operator hits 'Send issue' for a brand-new client, does the cockpit guarantee that client will open on a verdict the operator has actually made real — value set, segment confirmed, tracking confirmed — or can the single send button still ship a confident dollar headline backed by nothing because the setup lives one page away and the button only counts staged recs?

persona: Agency owner / operator — the economics lens. Cares about exactly two things: time saved per client per cycle, and dollars made (retention + expansion). Judges the cockpit by whether the operator spends curation time on what moves the client's number and catches the upsell moment, or on busywork the client no longer cares about.
currentFriction: The cockpit is BLIND to the very number the client now opens on. Grep of the entire src/components/strategy/ tree for outcomeVerdict / estimatedValue / IssueVerdict / outcomeValue returns ZERO. The operator curates an issue all day and never sees '14 new patients ~$11,000 vs $1,500 retainer' — the one sentence that decides renewal. They are flying the plane from a different cockpit than the one the passenger is sitting in.

Rec value is denominated in a meaningless unit. cockpitRowModel.ts valueTag() renders 'value 73' (Math.round(opportunity.value ?? impactScore)) as a blue data tag. The client now thinks in dollars and outcomes; the operator ranks moves by an abstract 0-100 score that no longer maps to anything the client sees. You cannot curate 'the moves that grow the number' when the queue won't tell you which moves touch the number.

The integrity-of-the-number work — the thing P1a says the operator now OWNS — lives entirely outside the cockpit, buried in Settings > ClientDashboardTab.tsx (1,124 lines). Outcome value, segment, GA4 event pinning, outcome-type mapping, and the Webflow webhook paste are all in a tab the operator visits once and forgets. The cockpit, where the operator actually spends time, gives ZERO signal that the number backing this client's verdict is unset, mis-set, or stale. A confident-but-wrong $11,000 ships with a green Send button.

StanceBar measures the wrong stance. It shows the archetype MIX (authority_bet/refresh/defend...) — an SEO-craft view of the issue. Post-reframe the question the owner needs answered is 'what is this issue worth to the client, and is that up or down since last cycle' — a money stance, not a craft stance. The operator optimizes for a balanced-looking bar instead of a growing dollar number.

Zero retention or expansion surface. Grep for retention/expansion/upsell/churn/renewal/MissedOpportunity/NextBets across the strategy tree returns nothing but a false hit. The spec literally enumerates expansion levers (call tracking, closed-loop attribution, do-the-work-for-you, next-bets-with-$) and maps them to client-side cards — but the OPERATOR, the only person who can actually pitch the upsell, has no surface telling them 'this client is at 7x ROI, now is the moment to propose call tracking.' The money moments are invisible to the money-maker.

The Send button has no economic conscience. IssueHeader's only gates are canSend (staged > 0) and 'everything already with client.' Nothing checks: is outcomeValue set? is the verdict positive or is this a down cycle the operator should narrate before sending? is tracking confirmed so the number is honest? The operator can one-click send an issue whose client-facing verdict is broken, empty, or embarrassing — and won't know until the client churns.

DraftedPovEditor curates PROSE about recommendations, not the dollar narrative. Situation / one-move / wins / flags are all rec-framed. The client's hero is now a dollar verdict, but the operator's narration tool doesn't ingest, reference, or pressure-test that verdict. The two surfaces have drifted: operator writes a craft story, client reads a money story.
reframeNeeds: A verdict header the operator sees FIRST — mirror the client's slot-1 sentence at the top of the cockpit ('This client: 14 new patients ~$11,000, 7x retainer, up from 9'), with the trend vs last cycle. The operator must curate against the same number the client opens on. This is the single highest-leverage change.

Dollar-denominated rec ranking. Re-express each rec's value tag from 'value 73' to projected $ impact (opportunity.value/estimatedGain x outcomeValue.valuePerOutcome), and sort the BackingMovesQueue by $ contribution by default. The operator should curate the moves that grow THE number, top-down, in the client's currency.

An integrity strip in the cockpit, not in Settings. A persistent readout at the top of the issue: 'Outcome value set (client-provided) · 3 events pinned · 2 typed · Webflow connected · last lead 2h ago' — green/amber/red. When the number backing the verdict is unset or stale, the cockpit blocks or loudly warns the Send. The operator owns the number's integrity, so the integrity status must live where they work.

A money stance, alongside (or replacing) the craft stance. StanceBar should answer 'is this issue's worth up or down, and what's driving it' — total projected value of staged moves, delta vs last cycle, and how much of the verdict each archetype is responsible for. Keep the mix view as a secondary toggle for SEO craft; lead with the money.

An expansion-moment surface for the operator. Where the spec puts MissedOpportunityCard / expand-attribution / next-bets on the CLIENT side, the cockpit needs the operator-facing mirror: 'This client clears 7x — propose call tracking ($X capturable in voicemail). 3 next bets worth ~$Y. They've shipped N wins — pitch do-it-for-you.' Surface the upsell where the person who pitches it actually is, gated on the trust guard holding (don't suggest upsell on a client whose current number is broken).

Send-with-economic-conscience. The Send gate must read the verdict: warn on a down/flat cycle ('verdict is flat vs last period — narrate it before sending'), block on a broken number (outcomeValue unset / tracking unconfirmed while measured_capture flag is ON), and surface the dollar the client is about to see in the confirm. Sending an issue is a financial act now, not a content act.

Segment-aware altitude in the operator's own view. The operator curating a board_vc client should see CAC-vs-paid framing; a local_smb operator should see calls+forms vs retainer. resolveSegmentProfile already exists server-side — the cockpit should consume it so the operator narrates at the altitude the client's verdict uses, not a one-size SEO view.
keep: The single canonical Send surface (Blocker 5). The word 'send' living in exactly one place, with staging everywhere else, is genuinely good operator discipline — it prevents accidental client writes. Numerator/denominator sharing one source (cockpitRecs + isCuratedForClient) is exactly right. Do NOT add a second commit path for the verdict; extend this one.

Staging-then-commit model with keep/cut/park/stage/edit verbs. Lets the operator compose the whole issue locally before any client write — fast, reversible, low-anxiety. This is the time-saver; preserve it intact.

The cut->POV-sentence contract (cutting a backing card removes its sentence live, staleness nudge on divergence). This co-design between curation and narration is the kind of integrity wiring the new direction needs MORE of — extend the same pattern to the verdict, don't break it.

Inline editing — edit wording (title/insight pencil) and edit POV prose with debounced optimistic PATCH. Operator control over the exact words the client reads is the agency's craft; keep it. Just add the dollar layer on top.

Config-as-page-chrome (StrategyConfigPanel mounted in IssueHeader, not tab-buried) and the inline disabled-reason on Send (not a tooltip). Both reduce operator friction and clicks. Right instincts — apply the same 'surface it where they work, explain it inline' philosophy to the new integrity/verdict needs.

Brand-law discipline already in place — teal=action, blue=data, emerald=wins, no purple, tokens only, shared ARCHETYPE_ACCENT so legend and queue dots match. Any reframe must inherit this, not regress it.
change: Add an IssueVerdictBar as the new slot-0 of the cockpit (above IssueHeader): renders the same server-assembled IssueVerdict / outcomeVerdict the client sees, plus trend vs last cycle and a basis/provenance chip (estimate_ga4 / measured_action). Read it from the existing computeROI().outcomeVerdict — no new endpoint. This is the anchor everything else curates toward.

Re-denominate CockpitRow's value tag and BackingMovesQueue sort to dollars. In cockpitRowModel.ts valueTag(), when outcomeValue exists, render projected $ (opportunity.value x valuePerOutcome, banded via fmtEstimate) instead of 'value 73'; default sortRecs to $ contribution. Keep the abstract score as a fallback only when outcomeValue is unset.

Add an IntegrityStrip to IssueHeader (or as a sibling above it) reading the same signals C-lane builds for ClientDashboardTab: outcomeValue set + basis, events pinned/typed, formCaptureConnected, conversionTrackingConfirmedAt, last lead time. Amber/red states link straight into the relevant setup; the Send button gates on it when the measured-capture flag is ON.

Give StanceBar a money mode: a second segmented bar (or toggle) showing each archetype's share of the staged issue's projected $ and the total delta vs last cycle. Lead the cockpit with money stance; demote craft mix to a toggle. Reuse the existing ARCHETYPE_ACCENT fills so it's a render change, not a new visual language.

Add an OperatorExpansionPanel (mirror of the client's expansion levers) in the collapsed lens region next to ContentWorkOrderLens/TrustLadderPanel: surfaces 'this client clears Nx ROI — propose X' moments computed from outcomeVerdict + tier + shipped wins, each as a transparent cost/return line. Gate every suggestion on the trust guard (suppress when the current number can't be trusted), exactly as the spec's expansion-moment design law requires.

Make Send economically conscious: extend IssueHeader's disabledReason/confirm to (a) show the dollar verdict the client will see in the send confirm, (b) warn on flat/down cycles, (c) block when measured-capture is ON but tracking is unconfirmed or outcomeValue is unset. One gate, conscience added — not a new path.

Feed the verdict into DraftedPovEditor: add a 'The number' beat (or pre-seed the lead sentence) anchored to outcomeVerdict, and reuse the existing staleness-nudge pattern when the narrated number diverges from the computed verdict. Co-design the operator's prose with the client's money story the same way cut->sentence already co-designs curation with narration.

Consume resolveSegmentProfile in the cockpit so verdict altitude, rec $-framing, and expansion suggestions match the client's segment (CAC-vs-paid for board_vc, calls+forms for local_smb). The resolver and public exposure already exist — this is a read, not new infrastructure.
thesis: Reframe the operator's job from 'curate a list of recommendations and write a point of view' to 'grow and guarantee a number, then narrate it' — the cockpit must surface the client's dollar verdict at the top, rank curation by dollar impact against it, prove the number's integrity (tracking/value setup) inline where the operator works, and flag the expansion moment when the trust guard holds. The operator should never be able to send an issue without seeing the dollar the client will see — and the queue should always tell them which move grows it.
killerQuestion: If you sat the operator and the client side-by-side, the client's screen opens on a dollar verdict and the operator's screen opens on an archetype-mix bar and a list of 'value 73' tags — so which screen is actually steering the number the business lives or dies on, and why is the person being paid to grow that number the one who can't see it?

persona: Strategist-storyteller (admin/operator) — the curator who owns the point of view and the narrative behind the number the client now leads with
currentFriction: I curate the issue blind to the verdict. The client's whole surface now opens on one dollar sentence ('N new patients ~$11,000 vs your $1,500 retainer'), but the cockpit (KeywordStrategy.tsx issueOverviewEl) has ZERO reference to outcomeValue, outcomeVerdict, or useClientROI. I shape Situation/'one move I'd bring'/wins/flags in DraftedPovEditor with no idea what number it sits beneath. I am narrating a story whose headline I cannot see.

My moves carry no dollar tag, so I can't tie curation to the number. CockpitRow renders only [severity][value][lifecycle] tags + a clamped why-line. The rec type ALREADY carries impactBand (client-safe banded monthly $ range) and opportunity.emvPerWeek/predictedEmv — the exact grammar of the verdict — but the row throws it away. I rank/keep/cut by archetype and a generic 'value' tag, never by 'this move is worth ~$X/mo toward the verdict.'

The 'next bets' card the spec scoped for P0 is missing, and it's MY lever. Spec line 316 scopes a NextBetsCard ('3 plays — here's what each could be worth') as P0 reframe-only; the client persona review flags it twice (lines 70, 99) as 'the one item that reads as quietly dropped.' It is the proactive-strategy, pay-more lever the operator personas would buy — and it's the storyteller's job — but neither the cockpit nor the client surface ships it.

StanceBar tells the wrong story now. It's a proportional bar of archetype MIX (authority_bet/refresh/defend...). That answered 'what kind of work am I sending' under a rec-denominated model. Under an outcome-denominated model the question is 'how much of the projected number does my staged set carry, and against what gap to the verdict.' Mix-by-archetype is craft-interesting but outcome-mute.

I can't see what the client will see before I send. Preview-as-client was explicitly REMOVED from IssueHeader (its own comment: 'removed as a dead control' because TheIssueClientPage didn't exist in Phase 1). So the single most important thing for a storyteller — read your own headline as the audience reads it — is impossible. I send the issue, then find out.

I own the integrity of the number but the cockpit gives me no readout of its health. P1a makes a measured verdict depend entirely on my setup (outcomeValue, pinned GA4 events typed to outcomes, the Webflow webhook, conversionTrackingConfirmedAt). A misset value or wrong pinned events = confident-but-wrong estimate the client can't sanity-check. Today that setup lives in Settings > ClientDashboardTab, a different surface entirely, with no signal back into the cockpit where I actually work the narrative.

My POV vocabulary is engagement/SEO-flavored, not outcome-flavored. 'Wins worth saying' and 'What I'd flag' are free prose with no binding to the dollar story; the only rec-linked sentence is the single lead move. The narrative and the verdict are two unconnected documents that happen to land on the same client screen.
reframeNeeds: The verdict, live, at the top of the cockpit. Surface the exact client-facing verdict sentence + provenance tier (estimate_ga4 / measured_action) + banded $ inside IssueHeader, read from the same useClientROI/outcomeVerdict source the client reads (extend, don't fork). I must narrate against the headline I can see.

A dollar tag on every move, sharing the client's source. Add a per-rec projected $ contribution to CockpitRow from the EXISTING impactBand.monthlyRangeUsd (client-safe) — never the raw admin-only emvPerWeek. Same banding (fmtEstimate, ~$X), same honesty discipline as the verdict, so the operator's per-move number and the client's headline can't drift.

A running 'staged set is worth ~$X toward the verdict' total. Replace/augment StanceBar's archetype-mix story with an outcome-denominated stance: sum the staged moves' projected $ and show it against the verdict gap. Curation becomes 'am I backing the number?' not 'is my archetype mix balanced?'

The NextBetsCard, built, as the operator's proactive lever. '3 plays — here's what each is worth' from rec impactBand re-expressed via outcomeValue. This is the storyteller's forward-looking column AND the documented P0 miss; it belongs in the cockpit (where I curate it) and on the client surface (where it earns expansion).

A number-integrity readout WHERE I curate, not buried in Settings. Pull the P1a verification readout ('3 events pinned · 2 typed · forms connected · last lead 2h ago' + provenance tier + a one-click 'value unset / events unpinned' warning) into the cockpit as a prerequisite banner. I own the number's integrity; the cockpit must show me its health before I send a confident-but-wrong issue.

Preview-as-client, restored. A toggle to read the exact client verdict + outcome count + my POV as the client sees them, before commit. Reading your own headline as the audience is non-negotiable for a storyteller; it was removed only because the client page didn't exist yet — it does now.

POV fields bound to the outcome story. Let 'the one move I'd bring' and 'wins worth saying' carry their projected $ and the outcome noun (the client's displayName/unitLabel), so the prose and the verdict are one document. A staleness nudge should fire not just when moves diverge, but when the staged $ no longer matches the POV's claimed lead.
keep: The single-canonical-send discipline (one 'Send issue' button; per-row/bulk only STAGE; numerator/denominator share cockpitRecs + isCuratedForClient). This honesty-of-action contract is exactly the discipline the client honesty spine rewards — extend it to dollars, don't break it.

DraftedPovEditor as an editable, regenerable narrated POV with the cut→sentence live contract and the lost-keystroke guard (reset keyed only on generatedAt). The narrated point of view IS the storyteller's craft surface; it's the right home to bind the outcome story into — keep the component, enrich its vocabulary.

The operator verb set: keep / cut-strike (cascade confirm) / park-throttle (resurfaces in Nd) / stage / inline edit wording / inline edit POV. This is the right curation grammar regardless of denominator.

The honesty engineering the client panel cited as the win — banded estimates (fmtEstimate), explicit provenance, no fabricated number, no false precision. Mirror it EXACTLY in every cockpit dollar surface (per-move tag, staged total, next bets). The operator must never see precision the client can't.
Add-a-recommendation (operator-authored rec into the queue). The storyteller minting a bespoke move is core; just make the minted move accept a projected $ so it joins the outcome story.

Config-as-chrome (StrategyConfigPanel mounted in IssueHeader, not tab-buried) and the archetype-grouped queue with shortlist-to-5 + 'show the rest.' Good information architecture — the integrity readout and verdict slot in as siblings of config chrome.
change: Add a Verdict slot to IssueHeader: render the live client verdict sentence + provenance badge (estimate_ga4 / measured_action) + banded $, sourced from outcomeVerdict on ROIData via useClientROI (same source the client reads). Place it directly under the title, above config chrome.

Add a projected-$ tag to CockpitRow as a fourth tag slot, from rec.impactBand.monthlyRangeUsd (client-safe, banded). Strip/guard against ever rendering raw opportunity.emvPerWeek (admin-only). Show '—' honestly when a rec has no band rather than fabricating.

Reframe StanceBar (or add a sibling 'StagedValueBar'): show staged moves' summed projected $ against the verdict / a target gap, with the archetype mix demoted to secondary. Outcome-denominated stance, not rec-denominated.

Build NextBetsCard in the cockpit: top 3 active/stageable plays each with its projected $ (impactBand via outcomeValue), editable framing, that the operator curates and that feeds the client surface. Resolve the P0 spec discrepancy in the same change.

Add a NumberIntegrityBanner to the cockpit (reuse the P1a verification readout): outcomeValue set? events pinned + typed? webhook confirmed (conversionTrackingConfirmedAt)? provenance tier? With a deep-link to ClientDashboardTab and a hard 'verdict will read $0 / count-only' warning when value is unset. Gate a confident 'Send issue' on a non-broken number.
Restore preview-as-client in IssueHeader (the now-buildable toggle to TheIssueClientPage) so the operator reads the verdict + count + POV as the client before commit.

Extend the POV contract: thread the outcome noun (displayName/unitLabel) + the lead move's projected $ into DraftedPovEditor's 'one move I'd bring' and 'wins worth saying'; extend the staleness nudge to fire when staged $ diverges from the POV's claimed number. Co-design the POV regenerate prompt to speak in outcomes/dollars, not impressions/positions.
Add a per-move outcome-noun preview to CockpitRow so the operator sees how a move reads in the client's outcome vocabulary, not just SEO archetype language.
thesis: The admin job must shift from 'curate a recommendation list and draft a POV over it' to 'curate the outcome NARRATIVE — see the exact dollar verdict the client will lead with, tie every staged move to its projected $ contribution toward that number, and own the integrity of the setup that makes the number provable.' The cockpit's job is no longer 'which recs do I send' but 'is my story, and the number under it, true and worth backing — and can I see both before I hit send.'
killerQuestion: If a client opened their dashboard right now, read '~$11,000 from your tracked conversions,' and emailed you 'which of the moves you're working drives that number, and what's the next one worth?' — could you answer from the cockpit without leaving it, or would you have to reverse-engineer a verdict you've never actually seen from an archetype mix and a generic value tag?
